_note: Hindsight / lead-time illustration for the 'before it lands' claim. Each measure was in the PUBLIC record — a published decree, an adopted national rule, or a tabled EU proposal — months before it took effect. Varsel reads that same public record, so the P&L exposure surfaces when a rule is proposed/published, not when it lands. This is NOT a claim that Varsel ran historically and predicted these (it didn't exist then); it shows the lead time INHERENT in the public record that a monitor like this gives you. Every date is public and cited (sources in docs/ceo-research-digest.md). Lead labels are the fixed signal→effect gap, so they don't go stale.

items.label: France — oral-nicotine ban
items.effectWhat: sale / import / possession ban in force
items.note: The category-ban trend was visible earlier still — Belgium banned 2023, the Netherlands Jan 2025.
